title: Vilfa humilis
taxonomy: Vilfa humilis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is commonly classified within the subfamily Pooideae. Within this subfamily, it is placed in the tribe Poeae. The genus Vilfa is part of the larger group of grasses known as the Poaceae family. Further classification of Vilfa humilis at the family and subfamily levels places it as a member of Pooideae within the Poaceae.

intro: Vilfa humilis, also known as the small-leaved Vilfa, is a perennial plant species in the grass family (Poaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its use in traditional remedies for wound healing, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Vilfa humilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Vilfa humilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat coughs, colds, and digestive issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits.
